english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Romans

verses.latin_vulgate: Debemus autem nos firmiores imbecillitates infirmorum sustinere, et non nobis placere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὀφείλομεν δὲ ἡμεῖς οἱ δυνατοὶ τὰ ἀσθενήματα τῶν ἀδυνάτων βαστάζειν καὶ μὴ ἑαυτοῖς ἀρέσκειν
verses.king_james: We then that are strong ought to bear the infirmities of the weak, and not to please ourselves.

verses.latin_vulgate: Unusquisque vestrum proximo suo placeat in bonum, ad ædificationem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἕκαστος γάρ ἡμῶν τῷ πλησίον ἀρεσκέτω εἰς τὸ ἀγαθὸν πρὸς οἰκοδομήν
verses.king_james: Let every one of us please *his* neighbour for *his* good to edification.

verses.latin_vulgate: Etenim Christus non sibi placuit, sed sicut scriptum est: Improperia improperantium tibi ceciderunt super me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ γὰρ ὁ χριστὸς οὐχ ἑαυτῷ ἤρεσεν ἀλλὰ καθὼς γέγραπται οἱ ὀνειδισμοὶ τῶν ὀνειδιζόντων σε ἐπέπεσον ἐπ᾽ ἐμέ
verses.king_james: For even Christ pleased not himself; but, as it is written, The reproaches of them that reproached thee fell on me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæcumque enim scripta sunt, ad nostram doctrinam scripta sunt: ut per patientiam, et consolationem Scripturarum, spem habeamus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅσα γὰρ προεγράφη εἰς τὴν ἡμετέραν διδασκαλίαν προεγράφη, ἵνα διὰ τῆς ὑπομονῆς καὶ τῆς παρακλήσεως τῶν γραφῶν τὴν ἐλπίδα ἔχωμεν
verses.king_james: For whatsoever things were written aforetime were written for our learning, that we through patience and comfort of the scriptures might have hope.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus autem patientiæ et solatii det vobis idipsum sapere in alterutrum secundum Jesum Christum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ θεὸς τῆς ὑπομονῆς καὶ τῆς παρακλήσεως δῴη ὑμῖν τὸ αὐτὸ φρονεῖν ἐν ἀλλήλοις κατὰ χριστὸν ἰησοῦν
verses.king_james: Now the God of patience and consolation grant you to be likeminded one toward another according to Christ Jesus:

verses.latin_vulgate: ut unanimes, uno ore honorificetis Deum et patrem Domini nostri Jesu Christi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα ὁμοθυμαδὸν ἐν ἑνὶ στόματι δοξάζητε τὸν θεὸν καὶ πατέρα τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: That ye may with one mind *and* one mouth glorify God, even the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter quod suscipite invicem, sicut et Christus suscepit vos in honorem Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ προσλαμβάνεσθε ἀλλήλους καθὼς καὶ ὁ χριστὸς προσελάβετο ἡμᾶς εἰς δόξαν θεοῦ
verses.king_james: Wherefore receive ye one another, as Christ also received us to the glory of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dico enim Christum Jesum ministrum fuisse circumcisionis propter veritatem Dei, ad confirmandas promissiones patrum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγω δὲ, ἰησοῦν χριστὸν διάκονον γεγενῆσθαι περιτομῆς ὑπὲρ ἀληθείας θεοῦ εἰς τὸ βεβαιῶσαι τὰς ἐπαγγελίας τῶν πατέρων
verses.king_james: Now I say that Jesus Christ was a minister of the circumcision for the truth of God, to confirm the promises *made* unto the fathers:

verses.latin_vulgate: gentes autem super misericordia honorare Deum, sicut scriptum est: Propterea confitebor tibi in gentibus, Domine, et nomini tuo cantabo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὰ δὲ ἔθνη ὑπὲρ ἐλέους δοξάσαι τὸν θεόν καθὼς γέγραπται διὰ τοῦτο ἐξομολογήσομαί σοι ἐν ἔθνεσιν καὶ τῷ ὀνοματί σου ψαλῶ
verses.king_james: And that the Gentiles might glorify God for *his* mercy; as it is written, For this cause I will confess to thee among the Gentiles, and sing unto thy name.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et iterum dicit: Lætamini gentes cum plebe ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πάλιν λέγει εὐφράνθητε ἔθνη μετὰ τοῦ λαοῦ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And again he saith, Rejoice, ye Gentiles, with his people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et iterum: Laudate omnes gentes Dominum: et magnificate eum omnes populi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πάλιν αἰνεῖτε τὸν κύριον πάντα τὰ ἔθνη καὶ ἐπαινέσατε αὐτὸν πάντες οἱ λαοί
verses.king_james: And again, Praise the Lord, all ye Gentiles; and laud him, all ye people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et rursus Isaias ait: Erit radix Jesse, et qui exsurget regere gentes, in eum gentes sperabunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πάλιν ἠσαΐας λέγει ἕσται ἡ ῥίζα τοῦ ἰεσσαί καὶ ὁ ἀνιστάμενος ἄρχειν ἐθνῶν ἐπ᾽ αὐτῷ ἔθνη ἐλπιοῦσιν
verses.king_james: And again, Esaias saith, There shall be a root of Jesse, and he that shall rise to reign over the Gentiles; in him shall the Gentiles trust.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus autem spei repleat vos omni gaudio, et pace in credendo: ut abundetis in spe, et virtute Spiritus Sancti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ θεὸς τῆς ἐλπίδος πληρώσαι ὑμᾶς πάσης χαρᾶς καὶ εἰρήνης ἐν τῷ πιστεύειν εἰς τὸ περισσεύειν ὑμᾶς ἐν τῇ ἐλπίδι ἐν δυνάμει πνεύματος ἁγίου
verses.king_james: Now the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, that ye may abound in hope, through the power of the Holy Ghost.

verses.latin_vulgate: Certus sum autem fratres mei et ego ipse de vobis, quoniam et ipsi pleni estis dilectione, repleti omni scientia, ita ut possitis alterutrum monere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πέπεισμαι δέ ἀδελφοί μου καὶ αὐτὸς ἐγὼ περὶ ὑμῶν ὅτι καὶ αὐτοὶ μεστοί ἐστε ἀγαθωσύνης πεπληρωμένοι πάσης γνώσεως δυνάμενοι καὶ ἀλλήλους νουθετεῖν
verses.king_james: And I myself also am persuaded of you, my brethren, that ye also are full of goodness, filled with all knowledge, able also to admonish one another.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audacius autem scripsi vobis fratres ex parte, tamquam in memoriam vos reducens: propter gratiam, quæ data est mihi a Deo,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τολμηρότερον δὲ ἔγραψα ὑμῖν ἀδελφοί, ἀπὸ μέρους ὡς ἐπαναμιμνῄσκων ὑμᾶς διὰ τὴν χάριν τὴν δοθεῖσάν μοι ὑπὸ τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: Nevertheless, brethren, I have written the more boldly unto you in some sort, as putting you in mind, because of the grace that is given to me of God,

verses.latin_vulgate: ut sim minister Christi Jesu in gentibus: sanctificans Evangelium Dei, ut fiat oblatio gentium accepta, et sanctificata in Spiritu Sancto.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰς τὸ εἶναί με λειτουργὸν ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ εἰς τὰ ἔθνη ἱερουργοῦντα τὸ εὐαγγέλιον τοῦ θεοῦ ἵνα γένηται ἡ προσφορὰ τῶν ἐθνῶν εὐπρόσδεκτος ἡγιασμένη ἐν πνεύματι ἁγίῳ
verses.king_james: That I should be the minister of Jesus Christ to the Gentiles, ministering the gospel of God, that the offering up of the Gentiles might be acceptable, being sanctified by the Holy Ghost.

verses.latin_vulgate: Habeo igitur gloriam in Christo Jesu ad Deum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔχω οὖν καύχησιν ἐν χριστῷ ἰησοῦ τὰ πρὸς θεόν
verses.king_james: I have therefore whereof I may glory through Jesus Christ in those things which pertain to God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim audeo aliquid loqui eorum, quæ per me non efficit Christus in obedientiam gentium, verbo et factis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γὰρ τολμήσω λαλεῖν τι ὧν οὐ κατειργάσατο χριστὸς δι᾽ ἐμοῦ εἰς ὑπακοὴν ἐθνῶν λόγῳ καὶ ἔργῳ
verses.king_james: For I will not dare to speak of any of those things which Christ hath not wrought by me, to make the Gentiles obedient, by word and deed,

verses.latin_vulgate: in virtute signorum, et prodigiorum, in virtute Spiritus Sancti: ita ut ab Jerusalem per circuitum usque ad Illyricum repleverim Evangelium Christi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν δυνάμει σημείων καὶ τεράτων ἐν δυνάμει πνεύματος θεοῦ ὥστε με ἀπὸ ἰερουσαλὴμ καὶ κύκλῳ μέχρι τοῦ ἰλλυρικοῦ πεπληρωκέναι τὸ εὐαγγέλιον τοῦ χριστοῦ
verses.king_james: Through mighty signs and wonders, by the power of the Spirit of God; so that from Jerusalem, and round about unto Illyricum, I have fully preached the gospel of Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sic autem prædicavi Evangelium hoc, non ubi nominatus est Christus, ne super alienum fundamentum ædificarem:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὕτως δὲ φιλοτιμούμενον εὐαγγελίζεσθαι οὐχ ὅπου ὠνομάσθη χριστός ἵνα μὴ ἐπ᾽ ἀλλότριον θεμέλιον οἰκοδομῶ
verses.king_james: Yea, so have I strived to preach the gospel, not where Christ was named, lest I should build upon another man's foundation:

verses.latin_vulgate: sed sicut scriptum est: Quibus non est annuntiatum de eo, videbunt: et qui non audierunt, intelligent.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλὰ καθὼς γέγραπται οἷς οὐκ ἀνηγγέλη περὶ αὐτοῦ ὄψονται καὶ οἳ οὐκ ἀκηκόασιν συνήσουσιν
verses.king_james: But as it is written, To whom he was not spoken of, they shall see: and they that have not heard shall understand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter quod et impediebar plurimum venire ad vos, et prohibitus sum usque adhuc.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ καὶ ἐνεκοπτόμην τὰ πολλὰ τοῦ ἐλθεῖν πρὸς ὑμᾶς
verses.king_james: For which cause also I have been much hindered from coming to you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc vero ulterius locum non habens in his regionibus, cupiditatem autem habens veniendi ad vos ex multis jam præcedentibus annis:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: νυνὶ δὲ μηκέτι τόπον ἔχων ἐν τοῖς κλίμασιν τούτοις ἐπιποθίαν δὲ ἔχων τοῦ ἐλθεῖν πρὸς ὑμᾶς ἀπὸ πολλῶν ἐτῶν
verses.king_james: But now having no more place in these parts, and having a great desire these many years to come unto you;

verses.latin_vulgate: cum in Hispaniam proficisci cœpero, spero quod præteriens videam vos, et a vobis deducar illuc, si vobis primum ex parte fruitus fuero.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς ἐὰν πορεύωμαι εἰς τὴν σπανίαν ἐλεύσομαι πρὸς ὑμᾶς ἐλπίζω γὰρ διαπορευόμενος θεάσασθαι ὑμας καὶ ὑφ᾽ ὑμῶν προπεμφθῆναι ἐκεῖ ἐὰν ὑμῶν πρῶτον ἀπὸ μέρους ἐμπλησθῶ
verses.king_james: Whensoever I take my journey into Spain, I will come to you: for I trust to see you in my journey, and to be brought on my way thitherward by you, if first I be somewhat filled with your *company*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc igitur proficiscar in Jerusalem ministrare sanctis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: νυνὶ δὲ πορεύομαι εἰς ἰερουσαλὴμ διακονῶν τοῖς ἁγίοις
verses.king_james: But now I go unto Jerusalem to minister unto the saints.

verses.latin_vulgate: Probaverunt enim Macedonia et Achaia collationem aliquam facere in pauperes sanctorum, qui sunt in Jerusalem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εὐδόκησαν γὰρ μακεδονία καὶ ἀχαΐα κοινωνίαν τινὰ ποιήσασθαι εἰς τοὺς πτωχοὺς τῶν ἁγίων τῶν ἐν ἰερουσαλήμ
verses.king_james: For it hath pleased them of Macedonia and Achaia to make a certain contribution for the poor saints which are at Jerusalem.

verses.latin_vulgate: Placuit enim eis: et debitores sunt eorum. Nam si spiritualium eorum participes facti sunt gentiles, debent et in carnalibus ministrare illis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εὐδόκησαν γάρ καὶ ὀφειλέται αὐτῶν εἰσὶν εἰ γὰρ τοῖς πνευματικοῖς αὐτῶν ἐκοινώνησαν τὰ ἔθνη ὀφείλουσιν καὶ ἐν τοῖς σαρκικοῖς λειτουργῆσαι αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: It hath pleased them verily; and their debtors they are. For if the Gentiles have been made partakers of their spiritual things, their duty is also to minister unto them in carnal things.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hoc igitur cum consummavero, et assignavero eis fructum hunc, per vos proficiscar in Hispaniam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦτο οὖν ἐπιτελέσας καὶ σφραγισάμενος αὐτοῖς τὸν καρπὸν τοῦτον ἀπελεύσομαι δι᾽ ὑμῶν εἰς τὴν σπανίαν
verses.king_james: When therefore I have performed this, and have sealed to them this fruit, I will come by you into Spain.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scio autem quoniam veniens ad vos, in abundantia benedictionis Evangelii Christi veniam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἶδα δὲ ὅτι ἐρχόμενος πρὸς ὑμᾶς ἐν πληρώματι εὐλογίας τοῦ εὐαγγελίου τοῦ χριστοῦ ἐλεύσομαι
verses.king_james: And I am sure that, when I come unto you, I shall come in the fulness of the blessing of the gospel of Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Obsecro ergo vos fratres per Dominum nostrum Jesum Christum, et per caritatem Sancti Spiritus, ut adjuvetis me in orationibus vestris pro me ad Deum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παρακαλῶ δὲ ὑμᾶς ἀδελφοί διὰ τοῦ κυρίου ἡμῶν ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ καὶ διὰ τῆς ἀγάπης τοῦ πνεύματος συναγωνίσασθαί μοι ἐν ταῖς προσευχαῖς ὑπὲρ ἐμοῦ πρὸς τὸν θεόν
verses.king_james: Now I beseech you, brethren, for the Lord Jesus Christ's sake, and for the love of the Spirit, that ye strive together with me in *your* prayers to God for me;

verses.latin_vulgate: ut liberer ab infidelibus, qui sunt in Judæa, et obsequii mei oblatio accepta fiat in Jerusalem sanctis,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα ῥυσθῶ ἀπὸ τῶν ἀπειθούντων ἐν τῇ ἰουδαίᾳ καὶ ἵνα ἡ διακονία μου ἡ εἰς ἰερουσαλὴμ εὐπρόσδεκτος γένηται τοῖς ἁγίοις
verses.king_james: That I may be delivered from them that do not believe in Judaea; and that my service which *I have* for Jerusalem may be accepted of the saints;

verses.latin_vulgate: ut veniam ad vos in gaudio per voluntatem Dei, et refrigerer vobiscum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα ἐν χαρᾷ ἔλθω πρὸς ὑμᾶς διὰ θελήματος θεοῦ καὶ συναναπαύσωμαι ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: That I may come unto you with joy by the will of God, and may with you be refreshed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus autem pacis sit cum omnibus vobis. Amen.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ θεὸς τῆς εἰρήνης μετὰ πάντων ὑμῶν ἀμήν
verses.king_james: Now the God of peace *be* with you all. Amen.
